# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI Workflow for NodeJS Project  # Name of your workflow, should be unique per repository and organization (optional)
on: [push]    # Triggers on push events to this repo or branch - optional  
jobs:     # Declare jobs in the same directory as .github/workflows. Use '.' notation for relative paths 
- nodejs      # Name of your job, should be unique per repository and organization (optional)   
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest       # Run on latest version of Ubuntu - optional  
  steps:     # Declare the step order in which jobs run. Use '.' notation for relative paths within a single workflow or across multiple files with '-' to separate them by job name and stage (optional)   
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2       # Check out your repo using GitHub Actions checkout action  
        with:         # Declare inputs in the 'with:' block. Use '.env.' notation for environment variables 
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node_version }}      # Set Node version to be used by this job (optional)   
       - name: Cache dependencies     # Name of your step, should match with above steps' names  
         uses: actions/cache@v2        # Use GitHub Actions cache action for storing and restoring cached packages. 
         with:          # Declare inputs in the 'with:' block   
           path: ~/.npm       # Path to restore caches from (optional)    
           key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}      # Key for cache storage, use '.env.' notation  
         run: npm ci --silent    # Run command in the step's environment - optional (--no-save flag is not used) 
       - name: Build and Test     # Name of your second job. Should match with above jobs names for dependencies to be cached here too!     
         uses: actions/setup-node@v2   # Set up NodeJS action, using the version specified in .github/.workflows file (optional)   
         with:          # Declare inputs within 'with:' block    
           node-version: ${{ matrix.node_version }}      # Use same as above step's input 
       - name: Test and Linting   # Name of your third job, should match to the one used in second for dependencies cache too!   
         run: npm test && npm run lint     # Run command within this environment (optional)
